rationale.summary: This PR makes the ST KillTimer timeout path tolerate the default empty callback while preserving callback execution when one is provided. The changed hunk keeps the timeout log and abort path intact, only guarding invocation of the optional std::function to avoid std::bad_function_call before the intended abort.

keyPoints.point: The timeout path now invokes the callback only when the std::function contains a target.
The existing timeout diagnostic and abort behavior are preserved around the guarded callback.

critique_ledger.title: Missing regression test for empty KillTimer timeout callback
critique_ledger.rationale: This bug fix changes the timeout path for the default-constructed empty callback, but the diff adds no test that would fail on the old code. A future refactor could remove the guard and reintroduce std::bad_function_call termination before the intended abort without CI catching it.

reasons: no blocking risk band, adherence failure, or L4-pending cohort
